description: Field-to-source mapping for the agent systems dataset. Only fields with a confirmed, accessible source are included.
verification_note: All URLs verified live on 2026-04-10 via WebFetch unless noted as training-knowledge.

system.name.notes: Homepage. OpenClaw binary locally verified at v2026.3.13.

system.license.notes: Check LICENSE file in GitHub repo.

qualification.persona.notes: Verified via openclaw --help: agents * subcommand, --profile flag, configurable agent defaults. Check docs for system prompt field details.

qualification.file_access.notes: Verified via openclaw --help: sandbox * and memory * subcommands. State stored under ~/.openclaw.

qualification.remote_accessibility.notes: Verified via openclaw --help: gateway *, acp *, channels *, devices *, webhooks *, dashboard, qr commands.

qualification.async_scheduling.notes: Verified via openclaw --help: cron *, webhooks *, node *, daemon * subcommands. Native cron scheduler confirmed.

comparison.cost_model.notes: Check homepage or pricing page. Open-source core likely free; verify if cloud/managed tier exists.

comparison.underlying_models.notes: Check docs for supported model providers and default model configuration.

comparison.tool_types.notes: Check docs for built-in tools. MCP support likely given modern agent framework — verify.

comparison.memory_types.notes: memory * subcommand confirmed in openclaw --help. Check docs for memory architecture details.

system.name.notes: Official Claude Code documentation. Redirects from docs.anthropic.com.

system.version.notes: Auto-updates on native install. Run claude --version for current version.

system.license.notes: Proprietary (Anthropic). Check legal page for full terms.

qualification.persona.notes: CLAUDE.md files define identity per project/folder. Confirmed in docs: 'How Claude remembers your project'. Multi-persona via separate CLAUDE.md files per business folder.

qualification.file_access.notes: Full local FS read/write confirmed. Tools: Read, Write, Edit, Glob, Grep, Bash. macOS, Linux, Windows (WSL) supported per install docs.

qualification.remote_accessibility.notes: Remote Control doc confirmed: continue local sessions from phone/any device. Claude iOS app, claude.ai/code web. Channels doc: Telegram, Discord, iMessage, webhook push. No public REST API for programmatic dispatch.

qualification.async_scheduling.notes: Desktop scheduled tasks (local, 1-min minimum interval) and cloud scheduled tasks (Anthropic-managed, 1-hour minimum) both confirmed in docs. Manual/hourly/daily/weekly/weekday presets plus natural-language scheduling via Claude.

comparison.cost_model.notes: Confirmed: average ~$6/dev/day, ~$100-200/dev/month with Sonnet 4.6 via API. Subscription tiers (Pro, Max) also available at claude.com/pricing. Check claude.com/pricing for current rates.

comparison.context_window.notes: Context window determined by underlying model (Claude Sonnet 4.6 default). Check Anthropic model docs for current limits.

comparison.tool_types.notes: MCP confirmed. Bash (code execution), Read/Write/Edit/Glob/Grep (file ops), WebFetch (web). Browser automation via MCP. Custom tools via Agent SDK.

comparison.memory_types.notes: CLAUDE.md (file-based persistent instructions), auto-memory (learned facts saved across sessions), in-context conversation history, external via MCP. No built-in vector store.

comparison.underlying_models.notes: Default: Claude Sonnet 4.6. Also Opus 4.6, Haiku 4.5 switchable via /model. Third-party providers (Bedrock, Vertex, Foundry) supported. Docs: /en/third-party-integrations.

system.version.notes: PyPI page shows latest version. Also: pip show crewai for installed version.

qualification.persona.notes: Agent(role, goal, backstory, system_prompt) confirmed in docs. Multi-agent crews with distinct personas. memory=True for persistent memory.

qualification.file_access.notes: Built-in FileReadTool, FileWriterTool, DirectoryReadTool confirmed via docs.crewai.com/llms.txt. Full local FS via Python stdlib.

qualification.remote_accessibility.notes: CrewAI Enterprise Cloud exposes REST API. crewai deploy CLI confirmed. Self-hosted via FastAPI wrapper.

qualification.async_scheduling.notes: Enterprise automations: webhook and API triggers confirmed. kickoff_async() for async execution. Cron scheduling via CrewAI Cloud Enterprise — verify exact primitives at docs.crewai.com/en/enterprise/guides/automation-triggers.

comparison.cost_model.notes: Open-source core is free (MIT). Enterprise/Cloud pricing at crewai.com/pricing — check for current tiers.

comparison.tool_types.notes: 40+ built-in tools confirmed. Custom tools supported. MCP: verify at docs.crewai.com — likely supported given ecosystem.

comparison.memory_types.notes: Unified memory system confirmed — replaces separate short/long-term/entity/external types. Hierarchical scopes, composite scoring (semantic + recency + importance).

comparison.underlying_models.notes: Confirmed providers: OpenAI (GPT-4, GPT-4o, o1, o3), Anthropic (Claude 3 family, Claude Sonnet 4), Google (Gemini 2.0-2.5), Azure, AWS Bedrock, Meta Llama, Groq, Mistral, Ollama, Hugging Face, IBM watsonx, Perplexity, Fireworks, SambaNova, Cerebras. Model-agnostic via LiteLLM.

qualification.persona.notes: Agent(name, role, description, instructions=[]) confirmed. System prompt fully configurable. Multi-agent teams with distinct personas.

qualification.file_access.notes: FileTools toolkit confirmed via docs.agno.com/llms.txt. Full local FS via Python stdlib.

qualification.remote_accessibility.notes: Remote execution docs confirmed: remote-execution/overview, remote-agent, gateway. FastAPI /run and /health endpoints. Agno Cloud Playground web UI. CLI available.

qualification.async_scheduling.notes: AgentOS scheduler confirmed: standard 5-field cron syntax, POST /schedules/{id}/trigger for manual trigger, retry logic, timezone support, run history. Event-driven/webhook not documented in scheduler — verify separately.

comparison.cost_model.notes: Open-source core free (MPL-2.0). Agno Cloud pricing at agno.com/pricing — check for current tiers.

comparison.tool_types.notes: MCP support confirmed via docs.agno.com/llms.txt (agent-os/mcp/tools). FileTools, custom tools confirmed.

comparison.memory_types.notes: In-context, external storage, persistent sessions confirmed. Vector store: check docs — Agno supports multiple backends.

comparison.underlying_models.notes: Model-agnostic. Check docs for full provider list — known to support OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, Groq, Ollama, and others.

system.name.notes: Official elizaOS docs. elizaos.github.io/eliza redirects → eliza.how → docs.elizaos.ai (verified redirect chain).

system.version.notes: npm package page for current version.

qualification.persona.notes: Character file fields confirmed: name (required), bio (required), username, system (custom system prompt), adjectives, topics, style, messageExamples, postExamples, knowledge, plugins, settings, templates. Most expressive persona system of all qualifying systems.

qualification.file_access.notes: Node.js runtime with full FS access. Plugin architecture allows file I/O via actions/services. No sandboxing by default.

qualification.remote_accessibility.notes: REST API confirmed in docs (rest-reference section). CLI (elizaos deploy) confirmed. Cloud deployment guide confirmed (guides/deploy-to-cloud). Docker deployable.

qualification.async_scheduling.notes: Background tasks doc confirmed: 'Long-running and recurring operations'. Built-in action loop runs continuously. Plugin-based event-driven triggers (Discord, Twitter, Telegram webhooks). Custom intervals per character. bootstrap plugin for scheduled actions.

comparison.cost_model.notes: Open-source, MIT. No pricing found in docs — self-hosted only for core framework. elizaOS cloud/managed tier: not confirmed, verify.

comparison.tool_types.notes: 90+ plugins confirmed. Plugin types: Actions, Providers, Evaluators, Services. Browser automation, code execution, custom tools all achievable via plugins. MCP: verify at plugin registry.

comparison.memory_types.notes: Memory doc confirmed: 'Give your agent a brain that remembers, learns, and evolves'. In-context, external storage, persistent memory all referenced. Vector store: check runtime/memory for backend details.

comparison.underlying_models.notes: Confirmed providers: OpenAI (gpt-4+), Anthropic (claude-3-opus+), Ollama (local: llama2, all-minilm). Plugin registry also shows Google GenAI, OpenRouter. Model-agnostic via unified API layer.

openclaw_cli_help.notes: Always-available local source for OpenClaw feature verification. Version: 2026.3.13.

crewai_llms_txt.notes: Complete CrewAI documentation index. Verified live 2026-04-10.

agno_llms_txt.notes: Complete Agno documentation index. Verified live 2026-04-10.

eliza_llms_txt.notes: Complete elizaOS documentation index. Verified live 2026-04-10.

claude_code_llms_txt.notes: Complete Claude Code documentation index. Verified live 2026-04-10.

comparison.context_window.max_tokens_per.reason: Context window is determined by underlying model for most systems. Sourced from model provider docs, not the framework docs. Will be populated in agent-systems.json using model-specific sources.
comparison.context_window.max_tokens_per.alternative_source: See underlying_models field and respective provider pricing/docs pages.

system.homepage_openclaw.reason: openclaw.dev timed out during verification. URL noted but not live-confirmed. CLI binary is the reliable source for OpenClaw.
